recalls.Description:
A recall has been issued for Aisstxoer adult bicycle helmets due to safety concerns that could lead to serious head injuries or fatalities. These helmets, designed for large head sizes (22.8 to 24.4 inches) and available in pink, feature black padding, straps, a buckle, and an adjustable knob. The packaging includes a white label with "Aisstxoer" and the size, while the model number "GH018L" is found inside the helmet itself.  
The helmets do not meet the required safety standards for impact absorption and stability, which significantly increases the risk of injury during accidents. Consumers are urged to take immediate action regarding this recall.
recalls.Title: Aisstxoer Adult Bicycle Helmets Recalled Due to Safety Risks of Head Injury; Sold by YXTDZ Store on Amazon
recalls.ConsumerContact: For more information, contact YXTDZ Store via email at yxtdzamz@126.com and visit the "Recalls and Product Safety Alerts" section on their website.

Hazards.Name: Non-compliance with mandatory safety standards for impact protection and stability, posing a risk of serious head injuries.

Remedies.Name: Consumers must stop using the helmets right away and reach out to YXTDZ Store for a full refund. They will need to destroy the helmet by cutting the straps and send a photo of the destroyed helmet to yxtdzamz@126.com.

recalls.Description: Specialized is recalling all Turbo Como SL electric bicycles due to a potential safety risk associated with the bike's fork. The recall affects all models of the Como SL, identifiable by the name printed on the seat tube or top tube. These bicycles were available in various colors, including Cast Umber, Dove Grey, Raspberry (Como SL 4.0), and Smoke, Brassy Yellow, and Brushed Silver (Como SL 5.0). To confirm the model, users can connect their bike to the Specialized app. Only the SL versions within the Como series are included in this recall.

recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach Specialized Rider Care at 877-808-8154 from 9 a.m. to 4 p.m. MT, Monday through Friday, email ridercare@specialized.com, or visit www.specialized.com for more details and to access the safety recall notices.

recalls.Description:
ProRider has issued a recall for several models of bicycle helmets due to safety violations that may lead to serious head injuries. The affected helmets include the Economy Bicycle Helmet, Bike Helmets with Turn Ring, Black Foam Bike Helmet, BMX Helmet, and Toddler Bike Helmet, available in colors such as blue, green, red, black, and purple. Each helmet features a label inside that displays the model number, manufacturing date, and serial number for identification.

The recalled helmets do not meet mandatory safety standards for impact attenuation and positional stability, which could result in inadequate protection during accidents. Consumers are urged to stop using these helmets immediately and follow the instructions for obtaining a refund.
recalls.Title: ProRider Bicycle Helmets Recalled Due to Serious Head Injury Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: For more information, contact ProRider at 800-642-3123 from 8 a.m. to 1 p.m. PT Monday through Friday, email org@prorider.com, or visit www.prorider.com and click on "Recall" at the top of the page.

recalls.Title: Peloton Recalls Original Series Bike+ Exercise Bikes Due to Fall Risks

recalls.Description: Knog is recalling the Blinder 900 and Blinder 1300 Front Bicycle Lights due to potential fire and burn hazards caused by overheating lithium-ion batteries. The affected products have batch codes: 0124AA, 0423AA, 0723AA, 0923AA, 1023AA, 1123AA, and 11A23AA, which can be found on the underside of the lights. These lights feature a black exterior and are branded with 'knog' and 'Blinder' on the handle. They were sold individually and in sets with a mount included. Proper disposal of the recalled batteries is crucial, as they pose a higher fire risk than regular batteries. Check with your local municipal hazardous waste center for safe disposal options for these batteries.
recalls.Title: Knog Recalls Blinder 900 and 1300 Bicycle Lights Due to Fire Risks
recalls.ConsumerContact: For more information, contact Knog at recall@knog.com or visit http://www.knog.com/recall. You can also access details by going to https://us.knog.com/ and selecting 'Blinder Recall' under the Support section.

These wheels were available as aftermarket products and also as original equipment for several premium bicycle brands. The brand name 'Apollo' is visible on the stem, while 'Apollo City' is marked on the rear left side of the deck's grip tape. The affected serial numbers include ranges from 4202240300001 to 4202240301236 and 4202240400001 to 4202240400764, which can be found on the bottom of the scooter deck or via the Apollo App.
recalls.Title: Apollo Recalls 2024 Electric Scooters Due to Fall and Injury Risks
recalls.ConsumerContact: Consumers should contact Apollo at 888-501-8862 from 9 a.m. to 5 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday, email support@apolloscooters.co, or visit https://apolloscooters.co/pages/apollo-city/recall for more information.

The battery's capacity, marked at 21.5Ah, along with the production date, can be found on a label on the front of the battery.

recalls.Description:
Transpro is recalling its A3 Hub Motor, A11F Spark, and R1 Commuter electric scooters due to safety concerns linked to unauthorized lithium-ion battery certification labels. These scooters, available in black or gray, have batteries located beneath the deck, posing a fire hazard. Consumers are advised against disposing of these batteries in regular trash or recycling bins and should seek proper disposal methods through local hazardous waste facilities.

To ensure safety, it is crucial to stop using and charging the affected scooters immediately. Transpro is offering a full refund or free replacement for these scooters. For disposal instructions and to initiate a claim, consumers can visit the Transpro recall website or contact them directly.

recalls.Description:
Bell Sports is recalling several models of children's and adult bicycle helmets due to a failure to meet federal safety standards, which increases the risk of head injuries during crashes. The affected models include the Axle, Cadence, Rev, and Frenzy helmets, identifiable by their model numbers B0605Y, B0605C, and B0494Y. These helmets were available in various colors and designs, including combinations of blue, green, pink, and more, with unique patterns such as checkers and polka dots. Consumers can find the model name, number, and manufacture date on a sticker inside each helmet.

Consumers are urged to stop using these helmets immediately and contact Bell Sports for a full refund. To ensure safety, consumers should cut the straps off the helmets before disposal. For refunds, photos of the destroyed helmets can be submitted to the provided email address. Bell Sports is also reaching out to known purchasers directly to address this issue.
recalls.Title: Bell Sports Recalls Axle, Cadence, Rev, and Frenzy Bicycle Helmets Due to Head Injury Risk

recalls.Description: Segway is recalling all models of the Ninebot Max G30P and G30LP KickScooters due to a potential fall hazard. The Max G30LP features a gray design with yellow accents, while the Max G30P is black with similar highlights. The brand name 'ninebot' is displayed on the foot platform and handlebars, with the model number found on a label on the foot deck. The Max G30P measures 46 inches in length, 19 inches in width, 47 inches in height, and weighs 42 pounds. These helmets, available in size small (S) and fitting head circumferences of approximately 19-1/2 to 21-1/4 inches, come in various color combinations including blue/pink, green/blue, and purple/pink/yellow. Retailers.Name: Target, Fred Meyer, Academy, and various other retailers and websites nationwide, sold from January 2023 through April 2023 for approximately $30.

Importers.Name: Bell Sports Inc., Irvine, California

Hazards.Name: Risk of head injury due to potential strap anchor dislodgement, violating CPSC safety regulations.

Remedies.Name: Consumers must stop using the helmets immediately and contact Bell Sports for a refund. To obtain a refund, destroy the helmet by cutting off the straps, email photos of the destroyed helmet to consumersupport-bell@bellhelmets.com, and then dispose of it.

recalls.metaDescription: Bell Sports recalls Slope adult bicycle helmets due to head injury risks. Stop using immediately and contact for refunds.

recalls.Description: Aventon is recalling its Class 2 Sinch.2 electric bicycles due to potential safety concerns. These folding e-bikes, equipped with a 500w motor, were available in two colors: silver (quicksilver) and blue (sapphire). Each bicycle features front and rear tires along with foldable handlebars, with the brand name "Aventon" displayed on the downtube and "Sinch.2" on the chain stay.
recalls.Title: Aventon Recalls Sinch.2 Folding E-Bicycles Due to Safety Risks
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, contact Ride Aventon at 866-300-3311 from 9 a.m. to 4 p.m. PT, Monday to Friday, or via email at info@aventon.com. Further details can be found online at https://www.aventon.com/pages/recalls or by visiting www.Aventon.com and clicking on "Recalls" at the bottom of the page.

Injuries.Name: The firm has received six reports of the recalled e-bikes accelerating unexpectedly. No injuries have been reported.

Retailers.Name: Available at bicycle retailers across the country and online at BestBuy.com and Eriksbikeshop.com from April 2023 to August 2023, priced around $1,800.

Importers.Name: Ride Aventon Inc., of Brea, California

Hazards.Name: Unexpected acceleration leading to loss of control, posing crash and injury risks.

Remedies.Name: Consumers should cease using the affected e-bikes and contact a Ride Aventon authorized dealer for a complimentary repair, which includes a software update.

recalls.metaDescription: Aventon recalls Sinch.2 Folding E-Bicycles due to safety hazards. Learn how to get a free repair and ensure your safety.

recalls.Description: DGL Group has announced a recall for specific Hover-1 Helix hoverboards due to a potential fire risk associated with the lithium-ion battery. The affected hoverboards feature serial numbers that include 15914, 19203, or 19988 as the fifth digit in the six-digit serial number located on the bottom of the device. These hoverboards are available in camouflage and galaxy colors, with 'Hover-1' displayed prominently on the front. The model name and serial number can be found on separate labels underneath the hoverboard.
recalls.Title: DGL Group Issues Recall for Hover-1 Helix Hoverboards Due to Fire Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: Consumers are urged to stop using the affected hoverboards immediately and reach out to DGL at 888-556-8426, available from 11 a.m. to 4 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday. Additional information can be found online at https://www.hover-1.com/pages/important-safety-information or by visiting www.hover-1.com and selecting 'Important Recall Information' at the top of the homepage.

Injuries.Name: DGL has received three reports of fire, which are associated with five reports of people experiencing headaches, nausea, or coughs from smoke inhalation and approximately $25,000 in reported property damage.

Retailers.Name: Available online at www.walmart.com and www.amazon.com from September 2020 to December 2021, priced between $170 and $200.

Importers.Name: DGL Group Ltd., of Edison, New Jersey

Hazards.Name: Risk of fire due to overheating lithium-ion battery

Remedies.Name: Contact Hover-1 for a free replacement hoverboard
